description_long: This is a mid-rise Parisian-style apartment building with four visible floors, featuring a mansard roof covered in dark, weathered tiles and multiple prominent chimneys. The facade is finished in a light, slightly worn plaster, with symmetrical rows of rectangular windows, some of which have black wrought-iron railings serving as balconies. The ground floor includes tall, narrow doors, and the upper floors are adorned with classic French window shutters and balcony railings. The building is divided into two parallel sections, separated by a narrow gap, each with its own set of windows and doors. The roofline is punctuated by several chimneys and skylights, adding to the historic European character. The overall appearance suggests a structure built primarily from masonry and concrete, with metal and glass elements for windows and railings.
description: A four-story Parisian apartment building with a mansard roof, light plaster facade, symmetrical windows, and wrought-iron balconies. The structure is divided into two parallel sections with classic French architectural details.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the plain back walls of both building sections, with minimal windows and visible weathering.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the narrow gap between the two building sections and highlights the vertical alignment of windows and doors.
description_view_2: The opposite rear view displays the unadorned back walls and the full extent of the roof's chimneys and skylights.
description_view_3: The front view showcases the main entrances, symmetrical window arrangement, and multiple balconies with wrought-iron railings.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns on each section, with regular spacing and alignment across all floors. Some windows feature wrought-iron railings, especially on the upper floors.
